diagnosis_name: Stress Hyperglycemia in Critically Ill Child

first_line_drugs.notes: Start 0.02–0.05 units/kg/hr IV infusion; titrate to maintain RBS 80–180 mg/dL
Use for dilution of insulin infusion (e.g., 1 unit/ml concentration); maintain IV fluids as per requirement

second_line_drugs.notes: Add if glucose falls <100 mg/dL or to maintain GIR in metabolic conditions

investigations.name: Blood Gas (ABG)
investigations.indication: To rule out acidosis (DKA vs stress hyperglycemia)

counselling: High blood sugar is common in critically ill children — गंभीर बीमार बच्चों में शुगर बढ़ना आम है
Insulin infusion will be used to control glucose safely — इंसुलिन इन्फ्यूजन से शुगर को सुरक्षित तरीके से नियंत्रित किया जाएगा
Frequent monitoring of sugar levels is required — बार-बार शुगर की जांच जरूरी है
Do not stop feeding unless advised — जब तक डॉक्टर न कहें, खाना-पीना बंद न करें
Condition will be closely monitored in ICU — ICU में हालत की करीबी निगरानी की जाएगी
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/endocrinologist if persistent hyperglycemia or suspected DKA
hospitalisation_criteria: All critically ill children with hyperglycemia requiring insulin infusion
notes: Do not reduce glucose infusion rate in metabolic conditions; instead add insulin to prevent catabolism
guideline_changes: Target glucose range relaxed to 80–180 mg/dL to avoid hypoglycemia risk
expected_course: Glucose stabilizes within 24–48 hrs with proper insulin titration
key_clinical_points: Avoid tight glycemic control to prevent hypoglycemia
Continue nutritional support (do not reduce GIR unnecessarily)

severity_assessment.severe: RBS >400 mg/dL or associated with acidosis/altered sensorium

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Hourly for first 4 hours, then 2–4 hourly
